channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources worldwide

item.title: 🇮🇷🇱🇧 Iran fights to preserve Lebanon as leverage in US settlement talks
item.description: Iran is waging a calculated campaign to keep Lebanon as its last bastion of Mediterranean influence, tying the country to a grand bargain with Washington as it seeks to end Hezbollah's war with Israel on its own terms.

item.title: 🛢️📉 Oil prices fall as Trump cancels planned Iran strikes, raising peace hopes
item.description: Oil prices settled lower after President Trump canceled plans to strike Iran, raising expectations for a deal to end more than three months of war and reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: 📈💰 Global markets rally on hopes for Iran peace deal
item.description: MSCI global equities index rallied while the dollar and bond yields fell on renewed hopes for Middle East peace after Trump canceled planned strikes against Iran.

item.title: 🇯🇵📊 Japanese business sentiment sours as Middle East war hits economy
item.description: Japanese business sentiment turned negative for the first time in four quarters, a government survey showed, reflecting growing economic pain from the Middle East conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🏛️ Trump to nominate Jay Clayton as Director of National Intelligence
item.description: President Trump plans to nominate Jay Clayton, the US Attorney for the Southern District of New York, as Director of National Intelligence, following Congress rejection of a FISA extension tied to spy chief appointments.

item.title: 🇮🇱 IDF Poised for Hezbollah Offensive in Nabatieh
item.description: Israeli military officials say armor and troops have been covertly moved into position for an attack on Hezbollah stronghold in southern Lebanon city of Nabatieh.

item.title: 🇨🇺 US Sanctions Cuba State Oil Company
item.description: The State Department announced sanctions against Cuba's state-owned oil and gas company Unión Cuba-Petróleo, expanding pressure on the island nation.

item.title: ⚔️ British Arms Dealer Convicted of Selling Fighter Jets to Warlords
item.description: A Londoner and Greek co-defendant were convicted of supplying planes, helicopters, guns, bombs, grenades and missiles to conflict zones around the world.

item.title: 🇬🇧 UK Defense Secretary John Healey resigns over military spending
item.description: UK Defense Secretary John Healey unexpectedly quit, saying the government is unwilling to spend enough on the military, in a fresh blow to Prime Minister Keir Starmer.

item.title: 💵 Dollar shaky as investors weigh rate outlook, Middle East worries
item.description: The dollar struggled as investors assessed global rate paths and monitored developments in the Middle East, where U.S.-Iran tensions have reignited.

item.title: 🌍 World Bank cuts global growth outlook to 2.5%, warns of drop to 1.3% if war fallout spreads
item.description: The World Bank lowered its 2026 global growth forecast to 2.5% and warned growth could drop to 1.3% if the Middle East war disrupts oil flows and trade more severely.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US House rejects short-term FISA extension as expiration looms
item.description: Congress rejected a short-term extension of foreign surveillance powers sought by Trump, deepening a standoff over his selection of an inexperienced loyalist as top spy chief.

item.title: 🌉 Gordie Howe Bridge connecting US-Canada opens, Trump attends ceremony
item.description: The new Gordie Howe Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor officially opened, with President Trump attending a ceremony marking improved US-Canada border infrastructure.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China joins Macron-led video call for rare economic talks ahead of G7 summit
item.description: French President Emmanuel Macron hosted a rare economic video call with China ahead of the G7 summit in France, as host nation seeks to project unity amid global trade tensions.

item.title: 🌍 Iran and Ukraine loom over G7 as France accommodates Trump
item.description: Wars in the Middle East and Ukraine are set to dominate next week's G7 summit, as host France crafts an agenda aimed at projecting unity and avoiding confrontation with Trump.

item.title: 🇨🇳 How Iran war is giving China opening in Gulf drone market
item.description: China is positioning itself to supply drones to Gulf states as regional security partnerships with the US face strain from the Iran conflict, opening new market opportunities.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia's central bank chief disappears from public view as resignation rumors swirl
item.description: Central Bank Governor Elvira Nabiullina has not been seen publicly in weeks, with one report linking her potential resignation to fears of border closures and martial law.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Saudi crown prince orders resumption of Lebanese exports
item.description: Saudi Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman ordered the resumption of Lebanese exports to the kingdom, a sign of warming ties between Riyadh and Beirut.

item.title: 🇸🇮 Slovenia revokes Netanyahu entry ban as ICC warrant standoff continues
item.description: Slovenia reversed its decision to ban Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu from an upcoming conference, amid pressure over ICC arrest warrant obligations.

item.title: ♟️ World Chess Federation suspends Russia over activities in occupied Ukraine
item.description: FIDE suspended the Russian Chess Federation over its continued activities in Russian-occupied Ukrainian territories, a rare sporting sanction linked to the war.

item.title: 🌍 UN's Guterres calls for return to US-Iran ceasefire
item.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres urged a return to the ceasefire between the US and Iran as fighting resumed, warning of broader regional consequences.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Graham says taking Kharg Island would be "ultimate game-changer"
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham said capturing Iran's Kharg Island oil terminal would be an "ultimate game-changer" in the conflict, potentially cutting off Iran's main oil export route.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Indian-crewed tanker hit off Oman in suspected third US strike this week
item.description: An Indian-crewed tanker was struck off Oman in what appears to be the third US strike on Iranian-linked shipping this week, as the conflict continues to affect global maritime trade.

item.title: 🇺🇸 Trump to be fan-in-chief or political showman at World Cup and other sporting events
item.description: President Trump is using a summer of sports tied to America's 250th anniversary to project power at home and abroad, staging events the administration calls "the greatest show on Earth."

item.title: 🌍 Why G7 is worried about global economic imbalances
item.description: France is using its G7 presidency to spotlight mounting global economic imbalances, as China's surging exports, chronic US deficits, and Europe's weak investment risk exacerbating trade tensions.

item.title: 🇮🇱 Israeli military says two 'launches' fall near Israeli troops in southern Lebanon
item.description: Israel's military said two launches were identified falling near Israeli troops operating in southern Lebanon, after sirens sounded in northern Israel amid ongoing tensions with Hezbollah.
